---
title: "4.14 Searching Algorithms"
subtitle: "Part 1: Linear Search"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit4-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Topic Intro

### Search means find a target

A <span class="term">searching algorithm</span> examines data to determine whether a target value is present, or where it appears.

This deck focuses on <span class="term">linear search</span>, which checks elements one at a time.

## Algorithm Idea

### Check each element from left to right

Linear search works on unsorted or sorted data.

```text
For each element:
    If the element equals the target:
        Return the index or true.
After all elements are checked:
    Return not found.
```

Worst case: the target is last or not present.

## Full Code

### Linear search with array index

```java
public static int linearSearch(int[] values, int target)
{
    for (int i = 0; i < values.length; i++)
    {
        if (values[i] == target)
        {
            return i;
        }
    }
    return -1;
}
```

Returning `-1` is a common signal for not found.

## Focused Trace

### Search for `9`

```java
int[] values = {4, 7, 9, 2};
int index = linearSearch(values, 9);
```

| `i` | `values[i]` | result |
|---:|---:|---|
| 0 | 4 | keep searching |
| 1 | 7 | keep searching |
| 2 | 9 | return `2` |

## ArrayList Linear Search

### Use `get(i)` and `size()`

```java
public static int linearSearch(ArrayList<String> list, String target)
{
    for (int i = 0; i < list.size(); i++)
    {
        if (list.get(i).equals(target))
        {
            return i;
        }
    }
    return -1;
}
```

Use `equals` for strings and other objects.

## String Search

### Search for a character or substring

```java
public static boolean containsA(String text)
{
    for (int i = 0; i < text.length(); i++)
    {
        if (text.substring(i, i + 1).equals("a"))
        {
            return true;
        }
    }
    return false;
}
```

String traversal is linear search over character positions.

## 2D Linear Search

### Search every row and column

```java
public static boolean contains(int[][] grid, int target)
{
    for (int row = 0; row < grid.length; row++)
    {
        for (int col = 0; col < grid[row].length; col++)
        {
            if (grid[row][col] == target)
            {
                return true;
            }
        }
    }
    return false;
}
```

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- explain linear search as checking elements one by one
- write linear search for arrays and `ArrayList`
- return immediately when the target is found
- place the not-found return after the loop
- adapt linear search to strings and 2D arrays
:::

## End

### Continue or return to the course outline

[Next: Binary Search](4-14-part-2-binary-search.html)  
[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
